THE COURT WAS PROVIDED WITH INSUFFICIENT FACTS NECESSARY TO EVALUATE THE SETTLEMENT. (CITY OF GRAND TERRANCE V. SUPERIOR COURT (1987) 192 CAL.APP.3D 1251, 1263.) THE COURT WAS UNABLE TO MAKE A ROUGH APPROXIMATION OF PLAINTIFF'S RECOVERY OR DEFENDANT 204'S LIABILITY. DEFENDANT 204 DID NOT PROVIDE THE COURT WITH SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFFS' ALLEGED HARM DID NOT OCCUR DURING THEIR OWNERSHIP OF THE SUBJECT PREMISES, OR THAT THEY HAVE ZERO LIABILITY FOR PLAINTIFFS' ALLEGED HARM AFTER DEFENDANT 204'S OWNERSHIP OF THE SUBJECT PREMISES EXPIRED. Hearing held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 204-11 LP HOLDINGS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement

is DENIED, without prejudice. The Court was provided with insufficient facts necessary to evaluate the settlement. (City of Grand Terrance v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1263.) The Court was unable to make a rough approximation of Plaintiff's recovery or Defendant 204's liability. Defendant 204 did not provide the Court with sufficient evidence that Plaintiffs' alleged harm did not occur during their ownership of the subject premises, or that they have zero liability for Plaintiffs' alleged harm after Defendant 204's ownership of the subject premises expired.
